summary: Validate the tail resource argument against tailKnownResources() and fail fast (non-zero) on an unknown name, before binding the poll loop.
reason: The generated tail command accepted any resource string and polled GET /<resource>, so an unknown/typo'd resource only ever 404'd while exiting 0 (and, outside dogfood, polled forever). The live-dogfood error-path probe (`tail __printing_press_invalid__`) expects a non-zero exit for an invalid argument. The permanent fix lives in the generator template (templates/tail.go.tmpl); this patch carries the same validation into the already-printed tree and becomes redundant after the next reprint.
validated_outcome: Full live dogfood gate green; `tail __printing_press_invalid__` now exits 1 with `unknown resource ...; known resources: [...]`.
